steps.title: Isolate the absolute value
steps.content: Divide both sides of $3|x - 7| = k$ by $3$: $$|x - 7| = \frac{k}{3}$$ For an equation of the form $|A|=c$, the number of solutions depends on $c$: positive gives two solutions, zero gives one solution, and negative gives no solutions.

steps.title: Find the value that gives one solution
steps.content: To have exactly one solution, the right side must be $0$: $$\frac{k}{3}=0$$ Then $|x-7|=0$, so the only solution is $x=7$.

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content: The only possible value of $\frac{k}{3}$ is $0$. The result matches.
